untranslatable_reason: Signal requires pattern recognition (EADB identification), measurement of formation length/width/height, and classification into median-based categories. These require candlestick pattern detection and geometric measurements not available in the allowed indicator set. The signal also lacks explicit entry/exit price levels, breakout thresholds, and reversal conditions. Success rates (37%/33%) and performance differences are statistical outcomes, not actionable trading conditions.
translation_notes: EADB is a chart pattern requiring visual/algorithmic identification not expressible with standard technical indicators. The signal references 'formation length between bottom lows,' 'narrow vs wide' (median-based), and 'short vs tall' classifications—all requiring pattern analysis. Pivot point entry/exit is a placeholder generic reversal logic. Recommend: (1) implement custom pattern detector for EADB, (2) calculate rolling median of pattern dimensions, (3) add explicit breakout levels relative to pattern bounds, (4) specify stop-loss relative to pattern height.
